title: Welcome to My Analytics Lab
text:
Hello! My name is Onur Can Gültekin.
I am an Industrial Engineering student at Hacettepe University.
This is my personal webpage.
Please stay tuned to follow my works on data analytics, blog posts, and more.

title: Assignment 1
text: My first assignment has two parts.

title: Assignment 1
text:
(a)
The video I choose is Veri Bilimi ve Endüstri Mühendisliği Üzerine Sohbetler - Mustafa Baydoğan & Erdi Daşdemir
Summary: Mr. Mustafa explains the main concepts related to data science and forecasting processes with real life applications. The talk focuses on data types, prediction models, the parameters affecting predictions and the consequences of these predictions in decision making processes. In addition, he also mentions ranking algorithms and the extent to which human contribution is still needed in the data and data prediction industry.

title: Assignment 1
text:
(b)
Question 1: Is human involvement still important in the data forecasting industry?
Answer: Yes, it is still important because human guidance is needed to interpret results, handle imperfect data and make better decisions based on predictions.
Question 2: What do we call a database format in which each row represents a single observation or example?
A) Structured Data (correct answer)
B) Unstructured Data
C) Random Data
D) Visual Data

title: Assignment 1
text:
Task 4

library(dslabs)
#I installed dslabs.

data("polls_us_election_2016")
#I imported the data from dslabs

my_first <- "Onur"
my_birth_year <- 2003
#Defined the variables

k <- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8
cat("k =", k, "\n")

k = 20 

if (k %% 2 == 0) {
  print(head(polls_us_election_2016, k))
} else {
  print(tail(polls_us_election_2016, k))
}

cat("Total number of NA values:", total_na, "\n")

Total number of NA values: 11604 

#I sorted the biggest 8 NA values from largest to smallest (I used GPT)

na_per_col <- sort(colSums(is.na(polls_us_election_2016)), decreasing = TRUE)
print(head(as.data.frame(na_per_col), 8))

                 na_per_col
rawpoll_mcmullin       4178
adjpoll_mcmullin       4178
rawpoll_johnson        1409
adjpoll_johnson        1409
grade                   429
samplesize                1
state                     0
startdate                 0

new_data <- polls_us_election_2016

for (col in names(new_data)) {
  if (is.numeric(new_data[[col]])) {
    #for Numeric Columns
    new_data[[col]][is.na(new_data[[col]])] <- my_birth_year + k
  } else if (is.character(new_data[[col]])) {
    #for character columns
    new_data[[col]][is.na(new_data[[col]])] <- paste0(my_first, "_", k)
  } else if (is.factor(new_data[[col]])) {
    # For factor columns
    levels(new_data[[col]]) <- c(levels(new_data[[col]]), paste0(my_first, "_", k))
    new_data[[col]][is.na(new_data[[col]])] <- paste0(my_first, "_", k)
  }
}

if (k %% 2 == 0) {
  print(head(new_data, k))
} else {
  print(tail(new_data, k))
}
